facade_colour: white-painted low commercial facade with bold black signboards and mural-painted lower walls

hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a purpose-built two-storey Commercial building that reflects vernacular interpretations of early-twentieth-century architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s pattern of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. The property has retained its original form and many building features including rug-brick cladding and second storey window openings with brick voussoirs and pediment. Anchoring the northeast corner of Augusta Avenue and Baldwin Street, the property supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. With storefronts that address both street facing elevations, the property defines the intersection of Augusta Avenue and Baldwin Street.

photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Facade is occupied and readable from the street, with contemporary storefront alterations layered onto the historic building form.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple fascia and coping with limited historic trim visible.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney clearly visible from the selected frontage view.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Decorative character is primarily expressed through the storefront treatment and surviving upper-storey massing.
photo_observations.storefront_description: Restaurant corner storefront with oversized illuminated signs and mural panels.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Storefront and signage have been modernized from the original historic condition.

photo_observations.window_details: Primarily a one-storey commercial frontage with multiple small street windows and signboards; any upper facade is largely hidden.
photo_observations.door_details: One corner glass door and one side commercial door are visible along the broad restaurant frontage.
photo_observations.signage_observed: Taquería El Pastorcito signs advertising tacos, burritos, quesadillas, tortas, beers, margaritas, and tequilas.

facade_description: 235 Augusta Ave presents a commercial street frontage within the Kensington Market HCD. The street frontage spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys beneath a flat, consistent with its commercial typology. The frontage is defined primarily by mixed in a white-painted low commercial facade with bold black signboards and mural-painted lower walls palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 3, 0 window bays from lower to upper levels, using storefront, and 2 principal entrances. Key storefront and facade features include cornice. HCD note: The property has value as a purpose-built two-storey Commercial building that reflects vernacular interpretations of early-twentieth-century architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s pattern of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. The property has retained its original form and many building features including rug-brick cladding and second storey window openings with brick voussoirs and pediment. Anchoring the northeast corner of Augusta Avenue and Baldwin Street, the property supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. With storefronts that address both street facing elevations, the property defines the intersection of Augusta Avenue and Baldwin Street.
